title: Koanophyllon porphyrocladum
taxonomy: Koanophyllon porphyrocladum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant is native to Mexico and belongs to the genus Koanophyllon. It is a member of the tribe Heliantheae and is further classified as a member of the subfamily Asteroideae. The classification of Koanophyllon porphyrocladum is based on its morphology, anatomy, and molecular characteristics. The specific classification of Koanophyllon porphyrocladum is a result of ongoing research and revisions in the field of plant taxonomy.

intro: Koanophyllon porphyrocladum is a plant species in the Asteraceae family, commonly known as a type of daisy or wildflower. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its reported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Koanophyllon porphyrocladum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat colds, flu, and other upper respiratory tract infections.
